notes.price_source: Tom Kovack confirmed NYBI26 prices (Feb 2026). Xactimate catalog codes from Al's NYBI8X_MAR26 extraction.
notes.description_source: Al's standardized Xactimate descriptions — these match carrier scope PDF wording exactly.
notes.install_vs_catalog: Install prices = material + labor + sub-contractor O&P (bundled). Catalog prices = material/remove only. Always use install prices for estimates.

Gable cornice strip - 3 tab - 2 stories or greater

items.supplement_argument: Ice barrier required per IRC R905.1.2 — minimum 24 inches from lowest roof edge past interior wall line. NY/PA/NJ mandatory in ice-forming regions
High-temp ice barrier required around chimney penetrations and high-heat areas per manufacturer specifications
Underlayment required under all asphalt shingles per IRC R905.1.1. Single layer minimum. Carrier cannot deny underlayment

items.description: Roofing felt - synthetic underlayment - Standard grade

items.supplement_argument: Starter strip required per shingle manufacturer installation instructions for warranty compliance. Eaves + rakes
Drip edge required at eaves and rakes per IRC R905.2.7. Minimum 2.5 inches below sheathing, 2 inches up deck. Underlayment OVER drip edge at eaves, UNDER at rakes. 5% waste factor standard

Step flashing required at all wall-to-roof intersections per IRC R905.2.8.4. Minimum 10 inches height. Siding removal mandatory for proper installation — nail horizontal flange to roof ONLY, never through siding
Counterflashing required at all roof-to-wall intersections per IRC R905.2.8
Pipe jack flashing must be replaced during re-roofing — existing boot seal is compromised during tear-off per IRC R905.2.8
Chimney flashing must be replaced during re-roofing per IRC R905.2.8. Existing step and counterflashing seal is broken during tear-off
Valley flashing required at all roof-to-roof intersections per IRC R905.2.8.3. Minimum 24 inches wide for open valleys

items.description: R&R Continuous ridge vent - shingle-over style
items.supplement_argument: Ventilation required per IRC R806 — 1 SF NFVA per 150 SF attic area (1:150 ratio). OC warranty VOID without proper ventilation. Ridge vent must be R&R during re-roofing
Damaged sheathing must be replaced per IRC R803.1 — minimum 1/2 inch CDX plywood for roof sheathing

items.supplement_argument: House wrap required behind all siding per IRC R703.2. Must wrap around outside corners per R703.1 — if carrier approves only 1 elevation, corner rule forces adjacent elevation replacement for continuous weather barrier
Include when fanfold insulation board confirmed present under existing siding
Siding labor minimum mandatory on every siding estimate

items.description: Scaffolding set up, and removal - charge per week
items.supplement_argument: Scaffolding required for safe siding installation — OSHA 29 CFR 1926.451 fall protection requirements
Shutters must be removed and replaced during siding work — include for every window with shutters on sided elevations
Step flashing removal required for proper reinstallation per IRC R905.2.8.4
Drip edge removal required during re-roofing — existing drip edge compromised during tear-off
Counterflashing removal required at all roof-to-wall intersections per IRC R905.2.8
Counterflashing required at all roof-to-wall intersections per IRC R905.2.8

items.supplement_argument: Fall protection required per OSHA 29 CFR 1926.501 for work at heights above 6 feet
Administrative labor for re-inspection and project coordination
Landscaping protection required during roofing operations to prevent property damage
Post-job cleanup required per contract and industry standard
Permit fees required per local building code for re-roofing
Job-related overhead — required for debris disposal
Separate dumpster for siding debris — cannot mix with roofing materials

Valley flashing required at all roof-to-roof intersections per IRC R905.2.8.3. Minimum 24" wide for open valleys

quantity_rules.ice_water_sf: Formula: (eave_lf * 3) + (valley_lf * 3 * 2) = SF. Per IRC R905.1.2, minimum 24 inches from lowest roof edge past interior wall line
quantity_rules.felt_sq: Formula: ((install_sq * 100) - ice_water_sf) / 100 = SQ. Covers roof area NOT already covered by I&W
quantity_rules.starter_lf: Formula: eave_lf + rake_lf = LF. Both eaves and rakes per manufacturer instructions
quantity_rules.drip_edge_lf: Formula: (eave_lf + rake_lf) * 1.05 = LF. 5% waste factor standard
quantity_rules.step_flashing_lf: Formula: step_lf * 1.05 = LF. 5% waste factor standard. Include ONLY when step_lf > 0
quantity_rules.ridge_cap_lf: Formula: ridge_lf = LF. Full ridge length
quantity_rules.gutter_lf: Formula: eave_lf * 1.6 = total gutter + downspout LF. Accounts for vertical downspout runs
quantity_rules.steep_charges: Pitch >= 7/12: apply to each facet group at that pitch tier. Both remove AND install charges. Tiers: 7-9/12, 10-12/12, >12/12
quantity_rules.high_charges: Stories >= 2: apply to FULL roof area. Both remove AND install charges
quantity_rules.shingle_waste: EagleView suggested %: 10% for 0-6/12, 12% for 7-9/12, 15% for 10-12/12, 17% for >12/12. Does NOT include ridge/hip/starter. Field complexity typically 15-18%

carrier_underpayment_patterns.description: Carrier omits or undercounts ice & water barrier at eaves/valleys
Carrier rolls starter into shingle price or omits entirely
Carrier ignores EagleView pitch data showing 7/12+ slopes
Carrier ignores story count for 2+ story properties
Carrier claims existing drip edge reusable after tear-off
Carrier denies separate underlayment line item
Carrier scopes fewer than 3 trades to avoid 10%+10% O&P
Carrier omits house wrap on siding claims
Carrier omits step flashing at wall-to-roof intersections
Carrier uses their own measurements instead of EagleView
Carrier applies no Law & Ordinance coverage for code-required items
